fix: disable Stylix nvf target and silence GTK4 warning

Two HM evaluation errors fixed:

home/features/theming.nix:
- Add stylix.targets.nvf.enable = false
  Stylix has a dedicated NVF target (modules/neovim/nvf.nix) separate
  from stylix.targets.neovim. With autoEnable = true it was setting
  programs.nvf.settings.vim.theme.name = 'base16', which conflicted
  with the 'gruvbox' theme in home/features/nvim/settings.nix.
  All three neovim-related targets (neovim, vim, nvf) are now
  explicitly disabled so NVF manages its own theme.

- Add gtk.gtk4.theme = null
  HM 26.05 changed the gtk.gtk4.theme default from config.gtk.theme
  to null. Setting it explicitly adopts the new default and silences
  the evaluation warning.

- Add { config, ... }: function signature (required for gtk.gtk4.theme)
- Expand target inventory comment to document gtk + the three NVF targets

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: MessagePort per-channel FX isolation (replace broken multi-output approach)

Replace multi-output AudioWorkletNode routing (which silently applied effects
to ALL channels) with MessagePort-based isolation:

- Main worklet renders isolation audio to heap buffers, sends via MessagePort
- IsolationPlayerProcessor receives audio and outputs through single output
- LibopenmptEngine.addIsolation() creates MessageChannel + player node
- ChannelRoutedEffects connects player node → effect chain → master
- Add try-catch for MessagePort transfer failures
- Remove stale workletNode guard that could abort rebuilds

Architecture: Main worklet → MessagePort → IsolationPlayerProcessor → effects

commit.message:
fix: eliminate cross-runtime deadlock — spawn_blocking + Handle::block_on

Replace nested current_thread runtime with Handle::current().block_on()
in all signal consumers (paper, live, alpha). This drives async futures
on the main tokio runtime's reactor instead of a separate runtime,
eliminating the cross-runtime lock contention that caused the portfolio
deadlock.

Also migrated signal channels from crossbeam to tokio::sync::mpsc and
fixed !Send issue by extracting portfolio lock before risk check.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(solvers): add CPU fallback for OOM during linalg.solve

When the 30k×30k normal-equation solve exceeds GPU memory,
transparently fall back to CPU for the factorization and move
the result back to the original device.  Applied to all three
solver paths: tikhonov, normal_equations (Cholesky), and
solve_from_normal_equations, plus StreamingNEFClassifier.solve().

The solve is a one-shot operation so the CPU overhead is minimal
compared to re-running the entire benchmark.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(governance): implement ISO 27001 Annex A and HIPAA Security Rule modules

Add six ISO 27001 Annex A control evaluators:
- Iso27001A912Control (A.9.1.2): access control policy / least privilege
- Iso27001A1011Control (A.10.1.1): cryptography policy / encryption
- Iso27001A1241Control (A.12.4.1): event logging / audit trail

- Iso27001A1323Control (A.13.2.3): electronic messaging / transmission security
- Iso27001A1813Control (A.18.1.3): protection of records / retention > 0 days
Iso27001ControlSet with evaluateRule(), isRuleCompliant(), generateReport(), collectEvidence()

- HipaaAccessControl (§164.312(a)(1)): required_roles non-empty
- HipaaEncryption (§164.312(a)(2)(iv)): require_encryption=true
- HipaaAuditControls (§164.312(b)): audit_access=true
- HipaaIntegrityControls (§164.312(c)(1)): audit_changes=true
- HipaaTransmissionSecurity (§164.312(e)(2)(ii)): export + encryption
- HipaaRetention (§164.530(j)): retention_days >= 2190 (6 years)
HipaaRuleSet with evaluateRule() and isRuleCompliant()

Add mutualism study analysis and game-specific instructions

  data format, figure panel mapping

fix(sf2): replace broken after-process callback with reliable setInterval polling

The after-process callback on _producerNode.onaudioprocess was not firing
because the ScriptProcessorNode is created lazily during first play, but
the callback was installed during engine init before the node exists.
With no setInterval fallback, position updates stopped entirely.

Fix: go back to simple setInterval(8ms) polling of sequenceIndex from
C64 RAM. 125Hz polling is fast enough to catch every row change. The
explicit rowDuration from currentSeqEventDuration provides consistent
visual timing via FormatPlaybackState smooth interpolation.

feat(bfs): use FalkorDB algo.bfs for BFS benchmark queries

Replace pure-Python BFS with FalkorDB's native algo.bfs() procedure.
Uses a set-difference approach to get nodes at exactly depth N:
  bfs(start, N) − bfs(start, N−1)

This ensures BFS traversal runs through the graph database rather
than bypassing it. Depth-1 queries use a single algo.bfs call.

Added execute_bfs() to graph_manager.py for reusable BFS execution.

Results: 550/550 BFS tests pass with F1=1.000, avg 0.22s/sample.

Fix/performance (#18)

* feat: large image stability & performance overhaul

Replace data URL IPC transport with binary buffers, add file-size and
pixel-count guardrails, move heavy image processing to a Web Worker
with latest-wins cancellation, and introduce progressive two-stage
rendering (fast preview + debounced full-res settle).

- Binary IPC: open/save paths use ArrayBuffer instead of base64 data URLs
- Guardrails: MAX_FILE_BYTES (25 MB) and MAX_PIXELS (40 MP) hard stops
- Worker: off-main-thread blur + threshold via typed-array pipeline
- Progressive: downscaled preview during slider drag, full-res on settle
- Memory: eliminate double image clone on load, lazy FloatingImage render
- Tests: new suites for useImageLoader and useImageProcessingWorker

* fix: make processed image widget fill container area and center image (#17)

The widget container (bg-white rounded-2xl shadow-lg) now fills the entire
available space (w-full h-full) instead of wrapping tightly around the image.
The image is centered within the widget. Outer padding (p-3) provides breathing
room from viewport edges for floating panels. Both the "no image" and "image
loaded" states share the same widget container with containerRef always attached
for proper ResizeObserver operation.

batch.patient_idx is per-graph [B] from PyG batching (scalar attrs are
stacked), NOT per-node [N]. Code was indexing with batch.ptr[gi] (a node
offset) which exceeds tensor size for any graph with >1 node.

* Fix noisy audio: rebuild Windows DLL with libvgmstream_setup PCM16 output

Previous commit bb7ea19 fixed Linux .so but missed the Windows .dll.
The user was running the old Windows DLL which output float32 (4 bytes/sample),
causing noise when Godot's AudioStreamWAV FORMAT_16_BITS tried to interpret
float bytes as int16.

Both binaries now rebuilt with libvgmstream_setup(force_sfmt=PCM16):
- libgodot_rocksmith.so: 5,376,000 bytes decoded (PCM16 confirmed, was 10,752,000 float32)
- godot_rocksmith.dll: rebuilt with same fix (was never updated after bb7ea19)

Verified on Linux sandbox:
  AudioEngine: decoded 496786 WEM bytes → 5376000 PCM bytes (2 ch, 48000 Hz)
  = 28s × 48000 × 2ch × 2 bytes (PCM16) ✓

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/santzit/go-guitar2/sessions/5adfc328-5224-4897-9f4c-d867421d6d9d
